agents.name: Planner
agents.lastMessage: Enhancing steric complementarity with SSTR2-unique ECL2/ECL3/TM5/TM6 regions by

report.hypothesis: Enhancing steric complementarity with SSTR2-unique ECL2/ECL3/TM5/TM6 regions by introducing bulky hydrophobic residues at positions 5 and 11 will increase selectivity margin (Δmargin > 0) by differentiating SSTR2 from off-targets like SSTR3/SSTR5.

agents.name: Critic
agents.lastMessage: The current design strategy is insufficient to achieve strong SSTR2 selectivity.

report.hypothesis: The current design strategy is insufficient to achieve strong SSTR2 selectivity. We must explicitly target non-pharmacophore positions to disrupt off-target interactions, particularly in SSTR3 and SSTR5, while preserving the core pharmacophore.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve selectivity, we need to disrupt conserved pocket contacts in off-targets SSTR3 and SSTR5. These positions are non-pharmacophore and can be mutated to reduce off-target binding while preserving the core pharmacophore (FWKT7-10 and Cys3/Cys14).

These mutations are designed to reduce off-target interactions without compromising the pharmacophore. For example, introducing hydrophobic residues at position 6 or polar residues at position 2 can disrupt conserved interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5.
